common.unknownError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

auth.title: Sign in to Quotio Admin
auth.description: Enter your admin API key to access the dashboard and manage your AI gateway.
auth.verificationFailed: Verification failed. Please check your API key.

shell.gatewayUrlHint: OpenAI-compatible base URL for your AI clients.

nav.overviewDesc: System health, usage, and real-time diagnostics.
nav.credentialsDesc: Manage API keys and provider connections.
nav.quotaDesc: Monitor usage limits and spending across providers.
nav.apiKeysDesc: Manage client credentials and usage analytics.
nav.logsDesc: Browse request history and delivery status.
nav.aboutDesc: Version, build info, and support details.
nav.providersDesc: Manage API keys and provider connections.
nav.agentsDesc: Manage setup and guidance for popular AI agents.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: A real-time snapshot of your gateway's health, traffic, and costs.
overview.incompleteDataDesc: Some data sources did not respond. Please refresh to try again.
overview.staleBanner: {{count}} data source may be outdated. Metrics shown are approximate.
overview.staleBanner_other: {{count}} data sources may be outdated. Metrics shown are approximate.
overview.providerHealthDesc: Connection status and credential count by provider.
overview.quotaSnapshotDesc: Current usage and limits by provider.

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Review aggregated request traffic, token usage, and cost trends from gateway logs.
logs.incompleteDataDesc: Log metrics did not return a complete payload. Refresh and try again.
logs.emptyDescription: Run requests through the gateway to start seeing log metrics and delivery trends.
logs.captureBodiesDesc: When enabled, Quotio stores request and response bodies for new log entries to support troubleshooting.
logs.noRequestBody: No request body data was captured for this entry.
logs.noResponseBody: No response body data was captured for this entry.
logs.noErrorData: No error payload was recorded for this entry.

columnsHelp.timestamp: When the gateway recorded this request log entry.
columnsHelp.endpoint: Gateway endpoint and transport method used for the request, such as POST /v1/messages or WS stream traffic.
columnsHelp.providerModel: Resolved upstream target in slug format. If a virtual model was requested, a mapping line shows requested model to resolved target.
columnsHelp.app: Client app identifier inferred from headers (X-Title, HTTP-Referer host, Originator, or User-Agent fallback).
columnsHelp.tokens: Token usage split into Input, Output, and Cache. Input = request input tokens, Output = generated tokens, Cache = cached input tokens reported by the upstream provider.
columnsHelp.cost: Estimated retail cost for this request based on known model pricing and parsed token usage.
columnsHelp.speed: Approximate generation throughput in tokens per second, computed from total tokens and request duration.
columnsHelp.finish: Finish signal extracted from upstream response payload, such as stop/completed/length.

models.title: Models
models.description: Manage discovered provider models, enablement, and sync state from one place.
models.emptyDescription: Sync a provider to populate the model catalog.
models.noMatches: No models matched the current search or filter.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Create and manage client keys, revoke instantly, and inspect usage.
apiKeys.emptyDescription: Create your first client key to start issuing bearer tokens.

create.title: Create API key
create.description: Set a label for this key. The plaintext token is shown only once.

created.title: API key created
created.description: This plaintext token is only shown once.
created.helper: Store it in your client now. It will not be returned by list, usage, or update requests.

rename.title: Rename API key
rename.description: Update display name without changing the secret.

usage.title: Usage details
usage.description: Inspect request volume, token consumption, and cost over time.
usage.noUsage: No usage matched this filter window.

messages.deleteConfirm: Delete {{name}}? This revokes the key immediately.
messages.statusFailed: Failed to update API key status

ampcode.title: AmpCode
ampcode.description: Configure Amp model mappings and management access control.

sections.cliSetupDesc: Apply or rollback Amp CLI local settings on the Quotio server host, and view manual setup instructions.
sections.upstreamAccessDesc: Configure Amp upstream API key and route priority mode. Providers remains the source of truth for execution credentials.
sections.modelMappingsDesc: Define exact mappings for predefined Amp slots and optional custom mappings.
sections.customMappingsDesc: Use this for non-predefined Amp model identifiers. Custom mappings only change model translation, not provider credentials.
sections.securityDesc: Configure access policy for Amp management routes.

fields.targetFilterPlaceholder: Search by provider, model, or virtual target
fields.upstreamApiKeyPlaceholder: Enter a new upstream key to replace current one
fields.upstreamApiKeyHelp: Used by Quotio when proxying to ampcode.com for upstream fallback or Amp-first routing. This is not a Provider credential.
fields.clientBearerHelp: AMP_URL and AMP_API_KEY in Amp CLI authenticate the client to Quotio. That bearer token must be accepted by the current management auth policy.
fields.clientApiKeysHelp: Top-level api_keys are the only configured client bearer tokens for Amp management auth and Amp CLI defaults. admin.api_key is admin-only.
fields.routingModeHelp: Controls route preference only. Provider credentials are still managed in the Providers screen.
fields.restrictLocalhostHelp: Use the remote socket IP only. Requests from non-loopback addresses are denied.
fields.managementAuthPolicyHelp: Controls authentication for Amp management proxy routes. This does not change provider authentication.

options.preferLocalThenAmp: Prefer Quotio models first, then Amp fallback
options.preferAmpUntilQuotaExhausted: Prefer Amp first, then fallback to Quotio on quota/rate/unavailable

labels.installedByQuotio: Quotio-managed Amp CLI settings are currently installed.
labels.notInstalledByQuotio: Amp CLI settings differ from Quotio-managed values.
labels.effectiveUpstreamURLHelp: If no custom upstream URL override is saved, Quotio defaults Amp management and upstream fallback traffic to https://ampcode.com.
labels.diffDialogDescription: Review proposed changes before applying Amp CLI setup on the server host machine.
labels.diffPreviewTruncated: Preview is truncated for performance. Full diff rendering will be restored in a later fix.
labels.noDiffChanges: No pending changes in Amp CLI config files.
labels.rollbackUnavailable: Rollback is unavailable because there is no saved manifest yet.
labels.ampLoginNotRequired: After file-based config or env vars are set, amp login is not required.

validation.duplicateWithPredefined: This From value collides with a predefined slot mapping.
validation.duplicateCustomFrom: Duplicate From value in custom mappings.

virtualModels.title: Virtual Models
virtualModels.description: Configure named virtual model chains that automatically route requests across provider/model targets.

global.title: Global routing
global.description: Enable or disable virtual model routing across the gateway.

list.title: Configured models
list.description: Create, rename, enable, and reorder virtual model fallback chains.

entries.emptyDescription: Add a provider/model target to start building this chain.
entries.noSearchResults: No targets matched the current search.
entries.noAvailableTargetsDescription: All available provider/model targets are already part of this chain.

empty.title: No virtual models yet
empty.description: Create a virtual model to define an ordered chain of concrete provider/model targets.
empty.disabledTitle: Enable routing to create virtual models
empty.disabledDescription: Turn on virtual model routing first, then create your first chain.

fields.name: Virtual model name
fields.nameHint: Use this model name in client requests to activate the chain.
fields.searchPlaceholder: Search models, providers, or virtual targets

dialogs.modelDescription: Virtual models give one stable name to an ordered set of concrete routing targets.
dialogs.addEntryDescription: Choose a provider/model target to append to {{name}}.
dialogs.deleteModelDescription: Delete {{name}} and all of its configured entries? This action cannot be undone.
dialogs.importDescription: Paste an exported virtual-model configuration JSON payload.
dialogs.exportDescription: Copy the current virtual-model configuration payload.

validation.invalidJson: The import payload must be valid JSON.

messages.toggleFailed: Failed to update virtual model routing.
messages.reorderUnavailable: Cannot reorder entries until all visible entries have stable IDs.
messages.resetComplete: Reset virtual models to the default empty configuration.

placeholder.comingSoon: This feature is coming soon. We're actively working on it.
placeholder.aboutDesc: Version info and system details will appear here.
placeholder.credentialsDesc: API key and provider credential management is next.
placeholder.logsDesc: Request logs and delivery history are on the way.
placeholder.modelsDesc: Model configuration and routing management is coming up.
placeholder.quotaDesc: Usage limits and spending controls will be available here.
placeholder.settingsDesc: Admin preferences and configuration options are in progress.

bootstrap.unknownError: An unexpected error occurred during startup.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Unified setup and guidance for Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Factory, and guide-only agents.
